steps.title: Use Desmos list evaluation
steps.content: The fastest check is to evaluate the function at all three table inputs at once. Enter $f(x)=39$ and then $f([0,1,2])$ in Desmos. The output is $[39,39,39]$, so every row must have output $39$.

steps.title: Understand the constant function
steps.content: Since $f(x)=39$ has no $x$ term, changing $x$ does not change the output. Whether $x=0$, $x=1$, or $x=2$, the value of $f(x)$ is always $39$.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong tables
steps.content: Choice A has outputs all equal to $0$, so it represents $f(x)=0$. Choice C has outputs $0,39,78$, which increase by $39$ each time and represent $f(x)=39x$. Choice D has outputs $39,0,-39$, which decrease by $39$ each time.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice B shows $f(0)=39$, $f(1)=39$, and $f(2)=39$. That exactly matches the constant function, so choice B is correct.

choices.text: • All three outputs are $0$, so this table represents $f(x) = 0$, not $f(x) = 39$. Eliminated.
• All three outputs are $39$, exactly matching the constant function $f(x) = 39$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
• Outputs are $0, 39, 78$ — each increases by $39$, so this represents $f(x) = 39x$, not a constant. Eliminated.
• Outputs are $39, 0, -39$ — each decreases by $39$, so this represents $f(x) = 39 - 39x$. Eliminated.
